text: Code reviews are a vital part of software development. They involve one or more developers checking a colleague's code before it becomes part of the main project. The main benefit is catching bugs early, which saves significant time and money compared to fixing issues later. Additionally, code reviews are excellent for knowledge sharing. Team members learn different approaches and coding styles, and junior developers gain valuable insights from more experienced colleagues. This process ultimately raises the overall quality of the team's work, ensuring that the final product is reliable and maintainable.
